% :alt: Supported Python implementations

The purpose of this package is to generate random (plausible) text sentences
and paargraphs based on a dictionary and a sample text. By default this package
will generate [Lorem Ipsum] style text, but you can customize the generator to
effectively load any dictionary and any sample text you like.

This package has en extensive docstring documentation, so you can read more on
the online documentation or in the python interactive shell as well.

[lorem ipsum]: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lorem_ipsum

modules.name: setup
documentation: python setup.py bdist_egg

modules.name: loremipsum
documentation:
This package provides a text generator class and some utility functions that
can simply return the text you desire. There are 2 sets of functions:

- Those with **generate\_** prefix that return the desired text and some stats
- Those with **get\_** that return the desired text without the stats

On the average, you probably want to import the **get\_** prefixed functions and
just get the text:

```pycon
>>> from loremipsum import get_sentences
>>>
>>> sentences_list = get_sentences(5)
>>> len(sentences_list)
5
>>>
```

If you fancy some statistics, you want to import the **generate\_**
prefixed functions:

```pycon
>>> from loremipsum import generate_paragraph
>>>
>>> sentences_count, words_count, paragraph = generate_paragraph()
```

If you need generate text based on your own sample text and/or dictionary, you
want to import the **Generator** class:

```pycon
>>> from loremipsum import Generator
>>>
>>> with open('data/sample.txt', 'r') as sample_txt
>>>     sample = sample_txt.read()
>>> with open('data/dictionary.txt', 'r') as dictionary_txt
>>>     dictionary = dictionary_txt.read().split()
>>>
>>> g = Generator(sample, dictionary)
>>> sentence = g.get_sentence()
>>>
```

modules.name: loremipsum.generator
documentation:
This module provides a simple way to generate "Lorem Ipsum" paragraphs,
sentences, or just random words.

functions.name: loremipsum.generator._paragraphs
documentation: Splits a piece of text into paragraphs, separated by empty lines.

functions.name: loremipsum.generator._sentences
documentation:
Splits a piece of text into sentences, separated by periods, question
marks and exclamation marks.

functions.name: loremipsum.generator._mean
documentation: Calculate the mean for a list of integers.

functions.name: loremipsum.generator._variance
documentation: Calculate the variance for a list of integers.

functions.name: loremipsum.generator._sigma
documentation: Calculate the sigma for a list of integers.

classes.name: loremipsum.generator.DictionaryError
documentation: The dictionary must be a list of one or more words.

classes.name: loremipsum.generator.SampleError
documentation:
The sample text must contain one or more empty-line delimited paragraphs,
and each paragraph must contain one or more period, question mark, or
exclamation mark delimited sentences.

classes.name: loremipsum.generator.Generator

methods.name: loremipsum.generator.Generator.__get_sentence_mean
documentation:
A non-negative value determining the mean sentence length (in words)
of generated sentences. Is changed to match the sample text when the
sample text is updated.

```
rtype

int

raises

 if value is lesser then 0
```

```
rtype

int

 if value is lesser then 0
```

methods.name: loremipsum.generator.Generator.reset_statistics
documentation:
Resets the values of {py:attr}`sentence_mean`,
{py:attr}`sentence_sigma`, {py:attr}`paragraph_mean`, and
{py:attr}`paragraph_sigma` to their values as calculated from the
sample text.

methods.name: loremipsum.generator.Generator.__get_sample
documentation:
The sample text that generated sentences are based on.

Sentences are generated so that they will have a similar distribution
of word, sentence and paragraph lengths and punctuation.

Sample text should be a string consisting of a number of paragraphs,
each separated by empty lines. Each paragraph should consist of a
number of sentences, separated by periods, exclamation marks and/or
question marks. Sentences consist of words, separated by white space.

```
param sample

the sample text

type sample

str

rtype

str

raises

 if no words in sample text
```

methods.name: loremipsum.generator.Generator.__set_sample
documentation: Set sample text.

documentation:
A dictionary of words that generated sentences are made of, grouped by

```
param words

list of words

type words

list

rtype

dict

raises

 if no valid words in dictionary
```

documentation:

if True, then the text will begin with the

type start_with_lorem

methods.name: loremipsum.generator.Generator.generate_paragraphs
documentation:
Generator method that yields paragraphs, of random length.

```
param start_with_lorem

if True, then the text will begin with the
standard "Lorem ipsum..." first sentence.

type start_with_lorem

bool
```

Generates random strings of "lorem ipsum" text.

Markov chains are used to generate the random text based on the analysis
of a sample text. In the analysis, only paragraph, sentence and word
lengths, and some basic punctuation matter -- the actual words are
ignored. A provided list of words is then used to generate the random text,
so that it will have a similar distribution of paragraph, sentence and word
lengths.

```
param sample

a string containing the sample text

type sample

str

param dictionary

a string containing a list of words

type dictionary

list
```
